{"signals":[{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"technical_instruction","confidence":0.96,"matched_text":"To generate a graph of the action intensity in a scene, you can use Python with the Matplotlib and Pandas libraries.","distilled_fact":"Python with the Matplotlib and Pandas libraries can be used to generate a graph of the action intensity in a scene."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"technical_instruction","confidence":0.92,"matched_text":"First, you need to create a dataset containing the intensity of the action for each second.","distilled_fact":"A dataset containing the intensity of the action for each second is needed first."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"example_dataset","confidence":0.99,"matched_text":"Let's assume you have manually assigned an intensity value (0 to 10) for each second of the scene.","distilled_fact":"The example assumes a manually assigned intensity value from 0 to 10 for each second of the scene."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"example_dataset","confidence":0.99,"matched_text":"action_intensity_data = [

    (2, 2),
    (3, 4),
    (4, 6),

    (6, 9),
    (7, 10),
    (8, 7),
    (9, 5),
    (10, 3),
    (11, 1),
    (12, 0)
]","distilled_fact":"The example action_intensity_data list contains the tuples (1, 1), (2, 2), (3, 4), (4, 6), (5, 8), (6, 9), (7, 10), (8, 7), (9, 5), (10, 3), (11, 1), and (12, 0)."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"code_object","confidence":0.97,"matched_text":"action_intensity_df = pd.DataFrame(action_intensity_data, columns=["Second", "Intensity"])

plt.ylabel("Action Intensity")
